title: How France's far-right RN lost a longstanding northern stronghold in the municipal elections
subhead: Villers-Cotterêts, a northern French town of 10,000 residents, had been governed by far-right mayor Franck Briffaut for 12 years. On March 22, independent centrist Jeanne Roussel took the town from the far right with 52.51% of the vote.

title: Trump welcomed in Beijing with pomp and a stern warning
Russian barrage pounds Kyiv, killing at least 24
A history of failed negotiations between Israel and Lebanon
subhead: In 1983, a peace agreement was signed between Israel and Lebanon but the Lebanese government annulled it just a few months later under pressure from Syria. Further bilateral negotiations took place between 1991 and 1993, following the Madrid Peace Conference, but yielded no results.

title: School uniforms have 'limited effects on student behavior and learning,' reveals French experiment
subhead: The experiment with a 'standard uniform,' launched in 2023, has involved about 100 elementary, middle and high schools. The first study on the measure highlights significant differences in perception between adults and students, and found 'modest and mixed' impacts on the school environment.

title: 2026 World Cup: France reveals 26-player squad
subhead: Coach Didier Deschamps on Thursday announced his squad for this summer's tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Kylian Mbappé will likely captain the team despite a thigh injury sustained last month.

title: Orphaned lynx released into the wild: The fight to save France's wildcats
subhead: The lynx, which disappeared from France at the beginning of the 20th century, is threatened by low genetic diversity, habitat fragmentation, road collisions and illegal killings.

How China is cracking down on its Christians
subhead: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

title: How one family and their shipping company dominate Marseille
subhead: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (3/4). Based in France's second-largest city, the company led by Rodolphe Saadé plays a major role in the city but has become less visible since the controversy over excess profits.

title: October 7: New report documents extent of sexual violence committed by Hamas
subhead: The most comprehensive report to date on October 7, with 400 witnesses and experts, finds that Hamas has 'deliberately and systematically resorted to sexual and gender-based violence.'
